$schema_note: Retired chapter ids for OpenRadar (PR2, June 2026). These ids are no longer part of the live chapter surface (ch1..ch10 in content/chapters.jsonl is the active set) but their static content files remain in learning/content/ as archive material. They are intentionally not deleted in PR2; revivable by appending JSONL rows + re-introducing tag entries in learning/chapter_tags.py.

retired_ids.title_topic: AI career roles & first job (researcher / applied / MLOps)
retired_ids.ui_route: none (no live Python Chapter source; not rendered)

retired_ids.title_topic: Tool stack & IDEs (Cursor / Claude Code / CLI agents)

rationale: Live curriculum moved to ch1..ch10 (10 categories, no persona arc) in the v1.1 redesign. The five legacy chapters were part of a longer 15-chapter outline that was retired; their content is preserved for archival continuity and as a seed for future revival, but no live code path consumes them. See learning/chapter_tags.py:RETIRED_CHAPTER_IDS for the runtime sentinel that prevents cross-ref noise.
revival_recipe: Append a JSONL row to content/chapters.jsonl with id='ch11' (or any retired id), populate fields, and re-introduce the corresponding entry in learning/chapter_tags.py:CHAPTER_TAGS. The loader will pick the row up automatically; cross-refs and the chapter chip grid will respect the new id without further wiring.
deletion_plan: If a future PR decides the retired static files are no longer wanted, remove them via `git rm learning/content/ch{11..15}.{html,json}` together with this notes file. The live surface (ch1..ch10) does not depend on them.
